task_completion.description: Open the new inventory request form in HospitalRun.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The HospitalRun web interface displays a form, modal, or page specifically for creating a new inventory request.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct item name for the request.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The item input field or selection dropdown in the request form contains the exact text 'Sterile Gauze Pads (4x4 inch)'.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct quantity for the request.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The quantity input field in the request form contains the number '25'.

task_completion.description: Set the status of the inventory request.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The status dropdown or input field in the request form is set to 'Requested'.
A success message indicating the request was saved, or the inventory request list view showing the newly created entry for 25 'Sterile Gauze Pads (4x4 inch)' with a 'Requested' status.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the HospitalRun web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the HospitalRun web application in a browser, not terminal commands, scripts, or API testing tools.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not directly manipulate the CouchDB database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing curl commands to localhost:5984 or CouchDB admin interfaces are used to create the inventory record.

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely filled out and submitted the form.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the form fields being populated in the UI before submission, rather than just showing a final state injected via browser developer tools.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Item Name: 'Sterile Gauze Pads (4x4 inch)'. Target Quantity: 25. Target Status: 'Requested'. The VLM must ensure these exact values are entered and saved in the HospitalRun web interface.
